name: Cinnamon-Cider-Cranberry Cake
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Cranberries (fresh or frozen), whole, rinsed, picked over, and dried
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F. Generously coat a 9-inch bundt pan with cooking spray, dust with flour, and tap out excess.
2. Sift together the flour, baking soda, cinnamon, and salt onto wax paper or into a bowl.
3. In another bowl, mix about 3 tablespoons of the flour mixture with the cranberries and toss well. Set aside.
4. In an electric mixer bowl, beat sugar, oil, and butter until blended. Add egg and beat well.
5. In a saucepan, bring cider or apple juice to boil. Remove from heat and add molasses, stirring until dissolved.
6. Alternately add the flour mixture and molasses mixture to the beaten mixture, beginning and ending with dry ingredients. Stir in cranberries. The batter will be thin.
7. Pour batter into prepared pan. Bake for 45-50 minutes or until springy to touch and a tester inserted in center comes out clean. Cool on wire rack for 15 minutes, then invert onto another rack and let cool.
LIGHT TOUCH:
For reduced calorie fat content, substitute 4 tablespoons hard stick corn oil margarine for the butter and oil (18% of total calories from fat).
Yield: 4 cups batter; one 9-inch bundt cake; 12 servings
Advance Preparation: Cake is best fresh from oven but may be made in advance, wrapped airtight, and frozen. Thaw and warm before serving.
Special Equipment: 9-inch nonstick bundt pan
Time/Temp: 350°F for 45-50 minutes (35 minutes was sufficient; cake was slightly overdone).
